props.name: Mtskheta
props.description: Ancient capital of the kingdom of Iberia (Kartli); religious center of Georgia.

props.name: Tbilisi
props.description: Capital of Georgia, traditionally founded in the 5th century.

props.name: Vani
props.description: Archaeological site of an ancient Colchian city, western Georgia.

props.name: Bolnisi
props.description: Town in Kvemo Kartli, site of the Bolnisi Sioni basilica.

props.name: David Gareja
props.description: Rock-hewn monastery complex on the border with Azerbaijan.

props.name: Gelati
props.description: Monastery and academy near Kutaisi founded in the 12th century.

props.name: Shota Rustaveli
props.description: 12th-century poet, author of 'The Knight in the Panther's Skin'.

props.name: Tamar of Georgia
props.description: Queen of Georgia, reigned 1184-1213, presided over the Georgian Golden Age.

props.name: David IV the Builder
props.description: King of Georgia, reigned 1089-1125, founder of Gelati Academy.

props.name: Vakhtang I Gorgasali
props.description: 5th-century king of Iberia, traditionally credited with founding Tbilisi.

props.name: Saint Nino
props.description: Traditionally credited with converting Iberia (Kartli) to Christianity in the 4th century.

props.name: Mirian III
props.description: King of Iberia who adopted Christianity as the state religion, c. 326 AD.

props.name: Ilia Chavchavadze
props.description: 19th-century writer and public figure, father of the Georgian national movement.

props.name: Vakhtang I of Iberia
props.description: Alternate historiographic naming for Vakhtang Gorgasali, used in some chronicle sources.

props.name: Christianization of Kartli
props.description: Traditional date c. 326 AD, under King Mirian III.

props.name: Founding of Tbilisi
props.description: Traditionally attributed to Vakhtang Gorgasali, 5th century.

props.name: Georgian Golden Age
props.description: Political and cultural peak under Queen Tamar, late 12th - early 13th century.

props.name: The Knight in the Panther's Skin
props.description: Georgian national epic poem, dedicated to Queen Tamar.

props.name: Kartlis Tskhovreba (The Georgian Chronicles)
props.description: Medieval corpus of Georgian historiographical texts.

props.name: Adishi Gospels
props.description: Old Georgian Gospel manuscript, dated 897 AD.

props.name: Bolnisi Sioni Inscription
props.description: Oldest securely dated Georgian inscription, 493-494 AD, in Asomtavruli script.

props.name: Ivane Javakhishvili Tbilisi State University
props.description: Maintains Georgian language and treebank corpora used in this lab.

props.name: National Centre of Manuscripts of Georgia
props.description: Custodian of Georgian manuscript heritage, including the Adishi Gospels.

props.description: Public research university in Tbilisi, Georgia, founded in 2006 from the merger of six academic institutions. Creator and host of the Kartvelian digital resource base used by this product.

props.name: Institute of Linguistic Studies, Ilia State University
props.description: Research institute of Ilia State University that develops and maintains the Georgian Language Corpus and the wider Kartvelian corpus, epigraphy, prosopography, manuscript, lexical, and NLP resources catalogued by this product.

props.note: Built from the Georgian Language Corpus (Ilia State University); distributed via Universal Dependencies.
